home.heroTitle: Africa: The Hidden Cradle of Christianity
home.heroDescription: Explore 2,000 years of unbroken faith. From the flight into Egypt to the rock-hewn churches of Lalibela, discover how the continent shaped and preserved the message of Christ.
home.jubileeDesc: We are preparing for the two-thousandth anniversary of the Redemption. A global celebration where Africa, rich in heritage and fervor, takes a central place.
home.mapPlaceholder: Discover the sites where biblical history met African soil.
home.ctaDesc: Your voice, your art, and your prayers are the living stones of this edifice. Join the Impronte Africane community to prepare for 2033.

bible.heroSubtitle: From the Garden of Eden to the early Church in the Book of Acts, the African continent is not on the sidelines of the Bible; it is one of its primary stages.

racines.title: Egypt: The Crucible of God's People
racines.p1: Africa is present from the dawn of humanity. The Gihon River, one of the branches of Eden, flowed through the land of Cush. It was in Egypt that Abraham found help, and it was on this same land that Jacob's clan became a numerous nation.
racines.p2: The stay in Africa forged Israel's identity. The pyramids and Egyptian wisdom were the setting for Moses' upbringing—'instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians'—before he received the Law at Sinai.
racines.p3: Africa is not just a temporary refuge; it is the soil where the promise made to the patriarchs took root and multiplied.
racines.quote1: Out of Egypt I called my son.
racines.quote2: The name of the second river is the Gihon; it winds through the entire land of Cush.

sagesse.title: Splendor of the South: Solomon and Sheba
sagesse.p1: The meeting between Solomon and the Queen of Sheba symbolizes the union between the revealed wisdom of Jerusalem and the sovereign dignity of Africa. The Queen of Sheba did not come as a mere spectator, but to test wisdom with her own enigmes.
sagesse.p2: This encounter left an indelible mark, particularly in Ethiopia, where tradition reports that this union gave birth to a royal lineage guardian of the Ark of the Covenant.
sagesse.p3: The Bible celebrates this woman 'who came from the ends of the earth' to seek the truth, becoming a model of spiritual quest for all humanity.
sagesse.quote1: When the Queen of Sheba heard about the fame of Solomon and his relationship to the Lord, she came to test Solomon with hard questions.

exil.title: Africa: Sanctuary of the Holy Family
exil.p1: When the Child Jesus was threatened by Herod's fury, Joseph was directed toward Africa. The continent became the protector of the Savior, offering asylum to the One who came to save the world.
exil.p2: This passage sanctifies Egypt and, by extension, Africa as a holy land, a place of divine preservation. Many monasteries along the Nile still mark the steps of this sacred journey.
exil.p3: Africa welcomed Christ in His fragile humanity, earning its place at the heart of the Redemption.
exil.cta: In the Footsteps of the Holy Family
exil.quote1: Get up, take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt.

premices.title: The Awakening of Africa: The Eunuch and Simon
premices.p1: From the earliest days of the Church, Africa responded to the Gospel. Simon of Cyrene, from present-day Libya, was the first to physically share the burden of the Cross with Christ.
premices.p2: Later, on the road to Gaza, the high official of Candace, Queen of Ethiopia, received baptism. He is the first convert of the nations mentioned in Acts, carrying the faith back to illuminate the South.
premices.p3: These firstfruits show that Africa did not wait for modern missions to embrace Christ; it was part of the first breath of the Holy Spirit.
premices.quote1: And the Ethiopian went on his way rejoicing.

event1.title: Simon of Cyrene
event1.desc: An African carries Christ's cross, becoming the symbol of the continent's participation in the Passion.

event2.title: St. Mark in Alexandria
event2.desc: The Evangelist founds the Church in Egypt, instituting one of the world's most prestigious theological schools.

event3.title: Felicity and Perpetua
event3.desc: Two young mothers martyred in Carthage, whose testimony rallied thousands to the faith.

event4.title: St. Athanasius of Alexandria
event4.desc: The 'Pillar of the Church' defends Christ's divinity at the Council of Nicaea, saving Christian orthodoxy.

event5.title: Kingdom of Aksum
event5.desc: Ethiopia becomes one of the first nations in the world to officially adopt Christianity as its state religion.

event6.title: Augustine's Confessions
event6.desc: The Bishop of Hippo (Algeria) publishes his masterpiece, influencing all Western thought for centuries to come.

event7.title: Lalibela Churches
event7.desc: King Lalibela carves eleven monumental churches from the rock, creating an African 'New Jerusalem'.

event8.title: Uganda Martyrs
event8.desc: Young courtiers give their lives for their faith, marking the spectacular renewal of the Church in sub-Saharan Africa.

event9.title: The Great Jubilee
event9.desc: Africa, the continent with the largest number of Christians in the world, invites the universal Church to celebrate 2,000 years of grace.

concours.title: Contest: Visions of the African Christ
concours.desc: How do you imagine the face of Christ in our cultures today? Participate in the great Jubilee 2033 artistic contest.
